Functional_role_1sentence: IL-13–responsive epithelial marker linked to mucus/secretory programs in asthma and used here to anchor the epithelial T2-high axis.
Notes: Open-access evidence for CLCA1–IL-13 relationship in pediatric asthma.

Functional_role_1sentence: Inflammation-associated epithelial marker used as part of the locked T2-high molecular axis; functional interpretation is limited to the T2-associated airway epithelial program.
Notes: Replace with asthma-specific SERPINB2 paper later if preferred; keep conservative wording.

Functional_role_1sentence: Mast-cell protease marker anchoring the signature to mast-cell effector biology relevant to allergic airway disease and remodeling.
Notes: Review-level is acceptable for S2; swap to CPA3-in-asthma primary study if desired.

Functional_role_1sentence: Chemokine receptor representing eosinophil trafficking axis implicated in Th2-mediated airway inflammation.
Histamine-synthesis enzyme anchoring the signature to mast-cell/basophil effector mediator biology in allergic airway responses.
Key_reference: Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2002. doi:10.1164/rccm.200206-619OC
Notes: Direct asthma-model evidence that HDC disruption reduces airway eosinophilia.

Functional_role_1sentence: Gel-forming mucin reflecting IL-13–driven goblet-cell/mucus program and pathological mucus hypersecretion in asthma.
Notes: Strong mechanistic support for IL-13–linked mucus program.

Functional_role_1sentence: Canonical type 2 cytokine anchoring the score to Th2/STAT6 signaling context; not interpreted as differential expression direction.
Notes: Classic overview of IL-4 roles in asthma; conservative anchor.

Functional_role_1sentence: Gel-forming mucin representing airway mucus composition and remodeling context; interpreted jointly with MUC5AC within asthma mucus biology.
Notes: Review covering both MUC5AC and MUC5B in asthma mucus dysfunction.

Functional_role_1sentence: Canonical type 2 cytokine that drives epithelial mucus programs and remodeling context in asthma; used as a pathway anchor for the epithelial component.
Notes: Using the same high-quality IL-13→mucus anchor keeps S2 consistent.

Functional_role_1sentence: Eotaxin-2 chemokine involved in eosinophil recruitment pathways, positioning the signature on an eosinophil-trafficking axis.
Notes: Directly supports CCL24-driven migration axis context.

Functional_role_1sentence: Matricellular protein widely used as a type 2–associated biomarker and remodeling-context readout in asthma; interpreted as ECM/remodeling component of the axis.
Notes: If you want a stronger mechanistic link (IL-13→POSTN), swap to a dedicated POSTN mechanistic paper later.
